topStories.title: FIIs and Mutual Funds increase stakes in Groww. Should You Buy, Sell, or Hold?
topStories.summary: Groww's parent, Billionbrains Garage Ventures, saw FIIs and mutual funds raise stakes in Q1FY27 as net profit surged 94% YoY, prompting Jefferies, JM Financial and Motilal Oswal to set a Rs 250 target price, implying about 21% upside from current levels.

topStories.title: Roads turn death traps for gig workers in Kerala
topStories.summary: Over 30 such workers, mostly food delivery personnel, have been killed in road accidents in the State in the past one year, as per informal data collected by a union. topStories.title: Hyderabad’s Forest & Fire food festival celebrates India’s indigenous cuisine
topStories.summary: From mahua mushroom bao and van shaak ravioli to saoji chicken, bamboo bhurji and junglee mutton, chef Harshita Kakwani’s Forest & Fire festival at Novotel Hyderabad Convention Centre celebrates India’s indigenous culinary traditions

topStories.title: Parliament Monsoon Session LIVE: Repeated adjournments disrupt both Houses; no business conducted so far
topStories.summary: Opposition parties are protesting for a discussion on question paper leaks and the student protests in Delhi's Jantar Mantar

topStories.title: Global Market: German bond yields hit two-year high as oil surge fuels ECB rate hike bets
topStories.summary: German bond yields rose as oil prices increased amid U.S.-Iran tensions. This surge strengthened expectations for the European Central Bank to tighten monetary policy. Money markets now anticipate higher deposit rates by early 2027. Traders have fully priced in a rate hike at the ECB's upcoming September meeting. Italy's 10-year bond yield also increased, widening the spread with German yields.

topStories.summary: The department formulated guidelines following a few unpleasant incidents in the State involving foreign tourists

topStories.title: HDFC Bank shares crash 5%, wipe off Rs 70,000 cr from investor wealth. Why Jefferies, Nomura, others see up to 28% upside?
topStories.summary: HDFC Bank shares fell sharply on Monday after its Q1FY27 earnings failed to impress investors, wiping out nearly Rs 70,000 crore in market value. Despite margin pressure and a muted market reaction, brokerages including Jefferies, Nomura, Motilal Oswal and Anand Rathi retained bullish ratings, seeing up to 28% upside in the stock.

topStories.title: Global Market: European shares dip as oil surge fuels inflation fears ahead of ECB meeting
topStories.summary: European shares dipped slightly as oil prices climbed due to escalating US-Iran tensions.  This surge revived inflation concerns and impacted investor sentiment ahead of key economic events.  Energy stocks performed well, while travel and leisure shares faced pressure from higher fuel costs.  Technology stocks saw gains as investors anticipated major US company earnings reports.  Markets also await the European Central Bank's policy decision this week.

topStories.title: India 10-year bond slumps to lowest in nearly a month as Middle East oil risks heat up
topStories.summary: Indian government bonds experienced a significant decline early Monday.  Escalating U.S.-Iran tensions pushed oil prices higher, impacting the rupee.  This situation prompted traders to reduce their positions in government bonds.  Higher crude prices strain India's import bill and government finances.  Foreign investor demand for Indian debt is expected to moderate following recent inflows.

topStories.summary: The UN health agency said travel restrictions create stigma

topStories.title: Goldman Sachs initiates coverage on Sansera Engineering, 3 other auto ancillary stocks with upside of up to 28%. Here’s why
topStories.summary: Goldman Sachs has initiated coverage on four Indian auto ancillary companies, turning bullish on Sansera Engineering and Craftsman Automation while assigning Neutral ratings to Bharat Forge and Samvardhana Motherson. The brokerage believes a shift towards precision engineering, aerospace, defence, semiconductors and EV-related opportunities will drive long-term growth and improve margins.

topStories.title: Indo MIM's Rs 3,812 cr IPO to open on July 23; price band set at Rs 461-485
topStories.summary: Indo MIM Ltd announced its Initial Public Offering, which opens for subscription on July twenty-third. The precision engineering components maker fixed a price band of Rs 461 to Rs 485 per share. This public issue comprises a fresh issue of equity shares and an Offer For Sale. The company plans to utilize Rs 400 crore from the fresh issue proceeds.

topStories.title: History test! What last 12 billion-dollar IPO listing gains indicate about SBI MF's Rs 9,813 crore debut
topStories.summary: SBI Funds Management is set to debut on the stock market on Tuesday, with an estimated 18% listing gain based on the current grey market premium. While its blockbuster subscription has raised expectations, history shows billion-dollar IPOs have delivered mixed outcomes. Winners like Eternal and ICICI Prudential AMC contrast with weak debuts by LIC, Paytm and Hyundai Motor India.

india.title: Six tiny fossil jaws found in Australia have pushed the origin of its carnivorous marsupials back by 5 million years
india.summary: A significant fossil discovery in Queensland has revealed Australia's oldest carnivorous marsupial. These ancient predators, dating back twenty-three million years, inhabited lush rainforests. The tiny fossil jaws belong to a newly identified species named Miyumba chrisdickmani. This finding fills a major gap in the continent's evolutionary history. It suggests dasyurids originated in wet habitats before adapting to drier landscapes.

india.title: Big Bear Eagles need $10 million to protect their habitat before July 31
india.summary: Thousands of people around the world are watching bald eagles Jackie and Shadow raise their two eaglets through a live nest camera in California's Big Bear Valley. As the chicks continue to grow, conservationists are also racing to raise $10 million to protect the surrounding habitat from development, highlighting the importance of preserving the forests and open spaces the eagles need to survive.

india.summary: Sally, a humanoid robot teacher, is set to debut at a New York high school, providing personalized educational experiences in specialized classes. Designed to be relatable with her realistic appearance and western New York accent, Sally will support both students and teachers with curriculum-aligned learning and translations. Additionally, the school has taken steps to reassure parents and the local community about the integration of artificial intelligence in the classroom.

business.title: FIIs and Mutual Funds increase stakes in Groww. Should You Buy, Sell, or Hold?
business.summary: Groww's parent, Billionbrains Garage Ventures, saw FIIs and mutual funds raise stakes in Q1FY27 as net profit surged 94% YoY, prompting Jefferies, JM Financial and Motilal Oswal to set a Rs 250 target price, implying about 21% upside from current levels.

business.title: Global Market: German bond yields hit two-year high as oil surge fuels ECB rate hike bets
business.summary: German bond yields rose as oil prices increased amid U.S.-Iran tensions. This surge strengthened expectations for the European Central Bank to tighten monetary policy. Money markets now anticipate higher deposit rates by early 2027. Traders have fully priced in a rate hike at the ECB's upcoming September meeting. Italy's 10-year bond yield also increased, widening the spread with German yields.

business.title: HDFC Bank shares crash 5%, wipe off Rs 70,000 cr from investor wealth. Why Jefferies, Nomura, others see up to 28% upside?
business.summary: HDFC Bank shares fell sharply on Monday after its Q1FY27 earnings failed to impress investors, wiping out nearly Rs 70,000 crore in market value. Despite margin pressure and a muted market reaction, brokerages including Jefferies, Nomura, Motilal Oswal and Anand Rathi retained bullish ratings, seeing up to 28% upside in the stock.

business.title: Global Market: European shares dip as oil surge fuels inflation fears ahead of ECB meeting
business.summary: European shares dipped slightly as oil prices climbed due to escalating US-Iran tensions.  This surge revived inflation concerns and impacted investor sentiment ahead of key economic events.  Energy stocks performed well, while travel and leisure shares faced pressure from higher fuel costs.  Technology stocks saw gains as investors anticipated major US company earnings reports.  Markets also await the European Central Bank's policy decision this week.

business.title: India 10-year bond slumps to lowest in nearly a month as Middle East oil risks heat up
business.summary: Indian government bonds experienced a significant decline early Monday.  Escalating U.S.-Iran tensions pushed oil prices higher, impacting the rupee.  This situation prompted traders to reduce their positions in government bonds.  Higher crude prices strain India's import bill and government finances.  Foreign investor demand for Indian debt is expected to moderate following recent inflows.

business.title: Goldman Sachs initiates coverage on Sansera Engineering, 3 other auto ancillary stocks with upside of up to 28%. Here’s why
business.summary: Goldman Sachs has initiated coverage on four Indian auto ancillary companies, turning bullish on Sansera Engineering and Craftsman Automation while assigning Neutral ratings to Bharat Forge and Samvardhana Motherson. The brokerage believes a shift towards precision engineering, aerospace, defence, semiconductors and EV-related opportunities will drive long-term growth and improve margins.

business.title: Indo MIM's Rs 3,812 cr IPO to open on July 23; price band set at Rs 461-485
business.summary: Indo MIM Ltd announced its Initial Public Offering, which opens for subscription on July twenty-third. The precision engineering components maker fixed a price band of Rs 461 to Rs 485 per share. This public issue comprises a fresh issue of equity shares and an Offer For Sale. The company plans to utilize Rs 400 crore from the fresh issue proceeds.

business.title: History test! What last 12 billion-dollar IPO listing gains indicate about SBI MF's Rs 9,813 crore debut
business.summary: SBI Funds Management is set to debut on the stock market on Tuesday, with an estimated 18% listing gain based on the current grey market premium. While its blockbuster subscription has raised expectations, history shows billion-dollar IPOs have delivered mixed outcomes. Winners like Eternal and ICICI Prudential AMC contrast with weak debuts by LIC, Paytm and Hyundai Motor India.

business.title: India office market set for stronger rental growth as consolidation gathers pace: Nuvama
business.summary: According to the report, India's gross office leasing declined 3 per cent year-on-year to 20.6 million sq ft in Q2CY26, as the US-Iran conflict affected corporate decision-making timelines

business.title: Warren Buffett admits to a rare mistake with these 2 big tech stock bets
business.summary: Warren Buffett admits he bought Alphabet too late and sold Apple too soon, even as Apple remains Berkshire's largest holding at nearly 22% of its portfolio. The 95-year-old also warns that today's market favours gambling over value investing.

business.title: CJP march: Forces lathicharge as protesters try to breach barricades near Parliament Street
business.summary: A heavy deployment of uniformed police personnel, along with officers in plain clothes, was in place across the area to maintain law and order and prevent protesters from moving towards Parliament

business.title: India Inc is returning to banks for money, but retail borrowers aren’t keeping pace
business.summary: India’s largest private banks are witnessing a shift in credit demand, with corporate and SME lending driving loan growth while retail borrowing remains subdued. Stronger working capital demand, refinancing and business banking boosted advances, but slower consumer credit growth and weaker CASA ratios continue to weigh on banks’ funding mix and margins.

business.title: US equity funds see first weekly outflow in three weeks as chip selloff weighs on sentiment
business.summary: US equity funds saw their first weekly outflow in three weeks, with a net $4.8 billion pulled amid a semiconductor selloff and US-Iran tensions, even as bond funds extended their winning streak to 13 weeks and money market funds recorded their largest outflow since April, per LSEG Lipper data cited by Reuters.

business.title: India has capacity for 8-9% growth; judicial, bureaucratic reforms key: Ex-CEA Subramanian
business.summary: Subramanian noted that India's macroeconomic foundations are strong and falling crude prices provide a near-term tailwind.

business.title: Monsoon Session: Lok Sabha adjourned till noon amid heavy Opposition sloganeering

business.title: Paytm likely to announce its first-ever bonus issue today: What lies ahead for its 7.5 lakh retail shareholders?
business.summary: Paytm's parent company will announce its first bonus share issue today. This announcement coincides with the release of its Q1 FY27 earnings report. Domestic investors have increased their stake in the fintech platform significantly. The company reported its first full-year profit in FY26. Global brokerages maintain a positive outlook on Paytm's stock performance.

business.title: Global Market: Citi downgrades South Korea to neutral as AI chip volatility mounts
business.summary: Citigroup has downgraded South Korea to neutral after a year-long overweight stance, citing heightened volatility in AI-linked chip stocks and stretched valuations. While remaining positive on the long-term AI theme, the brokerage retained an overweight rating on Taiwan and upgraded China, signalling a shift in emerging market positioning.

business.title: Global Market: ASML's AI-fuelled rally raises prospects of becoming Europe's first trillion-dollar company
business.summary: The artificial intelligence boom has propelled Dutch chip equipment maker ASML to become Europe's most valuable listed technology company, with investors increasingly betting it could become the continent's first trillion-dollar company. Strong AI-driven demand, a dominant position in advanced chipmaking equipment and bullish analyst forecasts have fuelled optimism, although geopolitical risks and sustained AI spending remain key variables.

business.title: BHEL share price: Brokerages see up to 23% upside after Maharatna PSU posts first Q1 profit in 8 years
business.summary: BHEL shares recently hit a 52-week high, but brokerages see more upside after the Maharatna PSU posted its first June-quarter profit in eight years. Strong execution, a robust Rs 2.6 lakh crore order book and improving margins have prompted ICICI Securities and JM Financial to reiterate their bullish outlook on the stock.

business.title: Tips Music shares jump 11% as board set to approve buyback on July 22

The announcement comes after the music label delivered strong FY26 earnings growth and continued to expand its music catalogue and digital audience.

business.title: Rupee weakens to two-month low as oil climbs; state-run banks cushion
business.summary: The Indian rupee reached its weakest point in two months on Monday.  Rising oil prices and Middle East hostilities pressured the currency's value.  State-run banks sold dollars, which helped to moderate the rupee's decline.  Higher crude prices can create macroeconomic turbulence for India's economy.  This situation impacts India's current account and foreign investment flows.

This large order strengthens its position in India's municipal wastewater sector. The projects will enhance sewage treatment capacity and water security goals. VA Tech Wabag's stock has shown strong performance over recent months.
